config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters a lot, but I do not think it should automatically take priority over individual rights, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by the state or by the majority. In my view, a healthy society protects basic rights first, then balances them with the common good when there is a real need. From my perspective, people are more willing to contribute to society when they feel their own dignity and freedoms are respected.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika mayoritas atau negara diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas individu, hasilnya sering justru penyalahgunaan, diskriminasi, dan pembungkaman suara yang tidak populer. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena dianggap lebih praktis atau lebih aman. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menuntut individu mengalah terus-menerus, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan pribadi sambil mencari kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia we have seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to silence criticism or justify unfair treatment. I understand the appeal of collectivist thinking, especially in a society that values harmony, but harmony without strong protections for the individual can become pressure to conform. The common good matters, but it should be built on rights that cannot be casually overridden by the majority or the state. For me, a healthy society is one that protects personal dignity first and then asks people to contribute to the collective from a position of freedom, not fear.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, justru ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi alasan terlalu luas atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” yang sering terjadi adalah pembatasan kebebasan, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan kuasa terhadap kelompok yang tidak populer. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dipakai untuk menginjak hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari perlakuan sewenang-wenang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling cepat memaksa individu tunduk, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand why people emphasize harmony and collective duty, but I have also seen how easily “for the public good” becomes a cover for silencing criticism or treating minorities unfairly. A society is stronger when it protects basic rights first, because then people can contribute without fear and without being forced to surrender their dignity. The common good matters, but it should be pursued within clear limits that prevent the majority or the state from overruling fundamental freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi kuasa terlalu besar atas nama kebaikan bersama, hasilnya sering berupa pembungkaman, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan wewenang terhadap orang yang tidak populer. Argumen tentang harmoni atau kepentingan umum memang terdengar masuk akal, tetapi tanpa batas yang tegas, itu mudah dipakai untuk mengorbankan kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan martabat pribadi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru melindungi hak dasar dulu, lalu mengejar kepentingan bersama tanpa memaksa individu tunduk sepenuhnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
